title: Welcome to the Global System of Trade Preferences
subtitle: A trade agreement designed by and for developing countries.
ni_description: As global trade grows more fragmented and protectionist, the GSTP gives developing countries a preferential channel to diversify export markets and build economic resilience. GSTP economies represent a combined $18 trillion market, accounting for 18% of global merchandise imports.
ni_description2: The agreement is also nearing a major milestone: the São Paulo Round, offering deeper tariff cuts across thousands of products, is just one ratification away from entering into force — a step that could unlock up to $14 billion in welfare gains for participating economies.
ni_def_statement_prefix: The Global System of Trade Preferences (GSTP) grants preferential tariff treatment among
ni_def_statement_suffix: participating developing countries, reducing tariffs on thousands of traded products.
ni_def_description: The GSTP is the only inter-regional trade agreement created exclusively by and for developing countries. Established in 1988, it expands South–South trade through preferential market access. The Committee of Participants is the GSTP's governing body, with UNCTAD serving as its Secretariat.
ni_def_attribution: UNCTAD, Global System of Trade Preferences among Developing Countries.
tl_description: For more than three decades, the GSTP has provided a framework for expanding trade among developing countries. Today, the Agreement is approaching an important milestone with the entry into force of the São Paulo Round – one additional ratification is required.

tl_milestones.label: One ratification away from entry into force

tr_groups.title: Economic welfare
tr_groups.description: The São Paulo Round could unlock substantial economic gains for developing countries — cutting tariffs across thousands of traded products and expanding preferential market access.

tiles.label: Products covered
tiles.description: Traded products subject to preferential tariff cuts under the São Paulo Round.

tr_groups.title: Global resilience
tr_groups.description: As the global economy becomes more uncertain, expanding South–South trade offers new opportunities to diversify export markets, strengthen resilience and foster sustainable economic growth.

tc_title: Intra-GSTP trade accounts for almost one quarter of the participating countries' exports
tc_subtitle: Share of exports to countries participating in the Global System of Trade Preferences, percentage, 2025

tc_legend.label: GSTP São Paulo Round Protocol ratifying party
Other GSTP São Paulo Round Protocol signatory

tc_source: UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) based on UNCTADstat.
tc_note: Intra-MERCOSUR trade is not counted in intra-GSTP trade. The value for MERCOSUR reflects the extra-MERCOSUR exports of its members (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay).
